intro: Craspedaria heterophylla, commonly known as the Himalayan or Korean craspedaria, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Asia, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Craspedaria heterophylla include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as poultices and decoctions.
